arguments.content:
$src = 'C:\Users\Scott-Pc\.clawdbot\agents\main\sessions'
$dst = 'C:\Users\Scott-Pc\clawd\chat-backups'
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dst | Out-Null
$ts  = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmmss'
$out = Join-Path $dst ("sessions-backup-$ts")
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $out | Out-Null
Copy-Item -Path (Join-Path $src '*.jsonl') -Destination $out -Force
Write-Output $out

**Implementing button styles**

I’m planning to apply a common class in globals.css or the component. I need to give the buttons a red outline with specific styles: border-[var(--brand-red)], bg-white, text-[var(--brand-red)], and hover:bg-red-50. 

Currently, the sidebar buttons use a neutral border, so I'll adjust those too, specifically on the tires page. The user is asking if I've made the buttons white with a red outline, likely considering them primary. I'll clarify and make those changes to the secondary buttons as needed.

**Editing button styles**

I need to edit the relevant files for the secondary buttons, particularly in src/app/page.tsx and the sidebar filter buttons on the tires and wheels pages. There are many occurrences to update, but I should focus on the sidebar buttons in the tires page.

content.text:
Yeah — the restart almost certainly nuked whatever was in-progress / not saved, which is why you’re not seeing the white buttons with the red outline.

I just re-applied it in **warehouse-tire-site** in a way that’s hard to “not take” again:

- Added a reusable global class: `.btn-outline-red` (white background + brand-red border + red text + light red hover)
- Applied it to the **sidebar “Apply …” buttons** on:
  - `src/app/tires/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/wheels/page.tsx`

And I committed it: **`b507a3c`** (“Style: red outline buttons; sidebar apply”).

If you still don’t see it, it’ll be one of these:
1) the site/server didn’t rebuild after the restart, or  
2) you’re looking at a different app/repo than `warehouse-tire-site`, or  
3) cache (hard refresh).

Tell me which sidebar buttons you mean beyond the “Apply …” ones (filter chips? nav links? admin sidebar?), and I’ll put the same `.btn-outline-red` look on those too.

arguments.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "Set-Location 'C:\Users\Scott-Pc\clawd'; Get-ChildItem -File -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Name -match '^current\.(png|jpg|jpeg|webp)$' -or $_.BaseName -eq 'current' } | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 10 FullName,Length,LastWriteTime"

content.thinking:
**Considering command syntax**

I’m thinking about whether the gateway replaces `$` sequences. It’s probably safer to use single quotes around the entire command for the `-Command`, right? I can do `-Command '& { ... }'` with single quotes from the command line. But in execution, I could just call it using `cmd /c` instead. For searching, I’ll use the command `dir /s /b current.*`, which should help me find what I’m looking for!

content.text:
I see exactly what you mean in the screenshot — the **“Apply price”** button in the sidebar is still showing the **neutral gray border**, not the **white w/ red outline** look.

Good news: in the code, those sidebar “Apply …” buttons **are** now using the new class:

- `src/app/wheels/page.tsx` has `btn-outline-red` on all those Apply buttons (I verified it’s in there).
- `src/app/tires/page.tsx` too.
- `src/app/globals.css` defines `.btn-outline-red`.

So if you’re still seeing the gray border, it’s almost certainly because the instance you’re looking at is running an **older build** (or the dev server didn’t restart/rebuild after the restart).

### Do this quick check
On the machine/server that’s running the site, from `warehouse-tire-site`:
1) restart the dev server (or rebuild/redeploy if prod)
2) hard refresh the browser (Ctrl+F5)

If you tell me whether you’re viewing **local dev** (`localhost`) or **a deployed URL**, I’ll tell you the exact command sequence (dev vs prod).

---

### Rename “Recommended Fitment” → “Sizing guide”
Agree 100% on the wording—“Recommended fitment” sounds like we’re endorsing custom-offset stuff.

I already changed the card title text:
- **Commit:** `040ead7`
- **Change:** `Recommended Fitment` → `Sizing guide` in `src/components/RecommendedFitmentCard.tsx`

If you want, I can also tweak the tiny disclaimer line underneath to be even more explicit (ex: “Sizing ranges shown are informational—final fitment confirmed before purchase/installation.”).
